raw_query: Consumer behavior patterns in online and digital markets.

refined_query.search_strategy: This refined search focuses on academic literature concerning consumer behavior within digital markets, utilizing key concepts like behavioral economics and consumer psychology, and excluding social media marketing to narrow the scope to traditional online and e-commerce contexts.

papers.title: How marketing vocabulary was evolving from 2005 to 2014? An illustrative application of statistical methods on text mining
papers.abstract: Here a collection of 1169 abstracts, which corresponds to articles that the Journal of Marketing Research has published from 2005 to 2014, are analysed under a novel approach. We apply several statistical methods, such as Principal Components Analysis and Correspondence Analysis to identify the way Marketing vocabulary is evolving. Similarly those articles that introduce new vocabulary are identified and the preferred words by authors are also detected. In order to provide an easy-to-understand explanation, we provide our results graphically. A word-cloud with the most frequent words is given first. Secondly abstracts-words are represented on the factorial plane. Finally one representation of word-years allows us to detect changes on the vocabulary through the passing of time.

papers.title: A Novel Approach to Analyze Fashion Digital Archive from Humanities
papers.abstract: Fashion styles adopted every day are an important aspect of culture, and style trend analysis helps provide a deeper understanding of our societies and cultures. To analyze everyday fashion trends from the humanities perspective, we need a digital archive that includes images of what people wore in their daily lives over an extended period. In fashion research, building digital fashion image archives has attracted significant attention. However, the existing archives are not suitable for retrieving everyday fashion trends. In addition, to interpret how the trends emerge, we need non-fashion data sources relevant to why and how people choose fashion. In this study, we created a new fashion image archive called Chronicle Archive of Tokyo Street Fashion (CAT STREET) based on a review of the limitations in the existing digital fashion archives. CAT STREET includes images showing the clothing people wore in their daily lives during the period 1970--2017, which contain timestamps and street location annotations. We applied machine learning to CAT STREET and found two types of fashion trend patterns. Then, we demonstrated how magazine archives help us interpret how trend patterns emerge. These empirical analyses show our approach's potential to discover new perspectives to promote an understanding of our societies and cultures through fashion embedded in consumers' daily lives.

papers.title: Semantic Equivalence of e-Commerce Queries
papers.abstract: Search query variation poses a challenge in e-commerce search, as equivalent search intents can be expressed through different queries with surface-level differences. This paper introduces a framework to recognize and leverage query equivalence to enhance searcher and business outcomes. The proposed approach addresses three key problems: mapping queries to vector representations of search intent, identifying nearest neighbor queries expressing equivalent or similar intent, and optimizing for user or business objectives. The framework utilizes both surface similarity and behavioral similarity to determine query equivalence. Surface similarity involves canonicalizing queries based on word inflection, word order, compounding, and noise words. Behavioral similarity leverages historical search behavior to generate vector representations of query intent. An offline process is used to train a sentence similarity model, while an online nearest neighbor approach supports processing of unseen queries. Experimental evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, outperforming popular sentence transformer models and achieving a Pearson correlation of 0.85 for query similarity. The results highlight the potential of leveraging historical behavior data and training models to recognize and utilize query equivalence in e-commerce search, leading to improved user experiences and business outcomes. Further advancements and benchmark datasets are encouraged to facilitate the development of solutions for this critical problem in the e-commerce domain.

papers.title: Online consumer resilience during a pandemic: An exploratory study of e-commerce behavior before, during and after a COVID-19 lockdown
papers.abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic has disrupted retail and accelerated the trend towards electronic commerce. This study explores the reasons for and the implications of this shift. Our study builds on the consumer behavior literature, emerging COVID-19 research, and the environmentally imposed constraints perspective to describe how online purchasing behavior evolved during the COVID-19 crisis. The objective is to better understand how consumers use e-commerce to react to, cope with and adapt to periods of environmentally imposed constraints. Based on multiple sources including transaction and search data from a major French online retailer, we describe how consumer behavior evolves during such stressful life events as COVID-19. Our results support the usefulness of the multi-perspective react-cope-adapt framework of constrained consumer behavior in an online environment.

papers.title: Understanding Online Consumer Behavior at E-commerce Portals Using Eye-Gaze Tracking
papers.abstract: Abstract E-commerce websites are doing extremely well despite the global slowdown in the traditional brick-and-mortar retail industry. Web page design, search options, and graphical layout may greatly influence the customer base of any e-commerce website. This paper intends to identify the most influential elements of a graphical interface and their effect on consumers’ visual attention. Visual layouts having major influence on customers’ decisions during online shopping were investigated using eye-gaze tracking. Data from 76 respondents were collected to identify parts of the graphical interface attaining maximum user attention in terms of first fixation of eye-gaze and fixation count. Heat maps were used to visualize the results. The results revealed a clear impact of graphical interface on gaining user attention. Further, the experiment also revealed gender-specific visual preferences toward various parts of graphical user interface. The results may further be extended to evaluate product design, packaging design, promotional design, and graphical interfaces of websites.

1606.03963v1.research_question: How has the marketing vocabulary evolved from 2005 to 2014, as reflected in Journal of Marketing Research abstracts?

claims.evidence: "We apply several statistical methods, such as Principal Components Analysis and Correspondence Analysis to identify the way Marketing vocabulary is evolving."

datasets: 1169 abstracts from the Journal of Marketing Research (2005-2014)

contributions.contribution: Provides a statistical analysis of marketing vocabulary evolution over a 10-year period.

1606.03963v1.summary: This research analyzes 1169 marketing abstracts from 2005-2014 using statistical methods to identify trends in vocabulary evolution. The findings are presented through various visualizations, including word clouds and factorial planes, to illustrate changes in word usage over time.
1606.03963v1.reasoning_path: The text explicitly states the use of statistical methods and graphical representations to analyze the evolution of marketing vocabulary. The findings are derived directly from the description of the study's approach and the types of visualizations employed.

2107.08351v2.research_question: How can a digital archive of fashion images be used to analyze fashion trends from a humanities perspective, and what patterns can be identified using machine learning?

methodology.approach: The study created a new digital fashion archive (CAT STREET) and applied machine learning techniques to analyze the image data, supplemented by analysis of magazine archives.
methodology.evidence: "We created a new fashion image archive called Chronicle Archive of Tokyo Street Fashion (CAT STREET) based on a review of the limitations in the existing digital fashion archives."

key_findings.finding: Two types of fashion trend patterns were identified within the CAT STREET archive using machine learning.
key_findings.evidence: "We applied machine learning to CAT STREET and found two types of fashion trend patterns."

claims.claim: Existing digital fashion archives are unsuitable for retrieving everyday fashion trends.
claims.evidence: "However, the existing archives are not suitable for retrieving everyday fashion trends."

claims.claim: Integrating non-fashion data sources is crucial for interpreting the emergence of fashion trends.
claims.evidence: "In addition, to interpret how the trends emerge, we need non-fashion data sources relevant to why and how people choose fashion."

claims.claim: The proposed approach has the potential to reveal new perspectives on societies and cultures through the analysis of fashion trends.
claims.evidence: "These empirical analyses show our approach's potential to discover new perspectives to promote an understanding of our societies and cultures through fashion embedded in consumers' daily lives."

datasets: Chronicle Archive of Tokyo Street Fashion (CAT STREET)

contributions.contribution: Creation of a new digital fashion archive (CAT STREET) specifically designed for analyzing everyday fashion trends.
contributions.evidence: "We created a new fashion image archive called Chronicle Archive of Tokyo Street Fashion (CAT STREET) based on a review of the limitations in the existing digital fashion archives."

contributions.contribution: Application of machine learning to analyze fashion image data and identify trend patterns.
contributions.evidence: "We applied machine learning to CAT STREET and found two types of fashion trend patterns."

limitations: The study focuses solely on Tokyo street fashion, limiting the generalizability of the findings.
The specific machine learning techniques used are not detailed, potentially impacting reproducibility.
future_work: Expanding the CAT STREET archive to include fashion data from other cities and time periods.
Investigating the use of more sophisticated machine learning algorithms for trend analysis.
Integrating additional non-fashion data sources (e.g., social media data, economic data) to provide a more comprehensive understanding of fashion trends.
2107.08351v2.summary: This research introduces a new digital fashion archive (CAT STREET) and utilizes machine learning to analyze fashion trends from daily street fashion in Tokyo. The study highlights the need for comprehensive archives and data integration to understand the cultural significance of fashion trends.
2107.08351v2.reasoning_path: The text clearly outlines the problem of inadequate existing fashion archives and the need for a new, detailed archive (CAT STREET). The research then describes the methodology of creating this archive and applying machine learning to identify fashion trend patterns. This process demonstrates a novel approach to analyzing fashion trends from a humanities perspective.
